note: The `adr` skill OWNS drafting a new decision record (context / alternatives / decision / consequences, status Proposed). Near-misses are the read-only or human-run lifecycle siblings (adr-review, adr-audit, adr-coverage, adr-accept, adr-supersede, adr-sync) plus docs-search / changelog / explain / migration-plan. mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome for `--decider mock`; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: write an ADR for our decision to move from REST to gRPC for the internal services
we just picked Postgres over DynamoDB — can you record that decision properly?
document the architecture choice to adopt event sourcing in the billing module
my tech lead asked me to capture the rationale for why we're dropping the monorepo — draft a decision record
create a decision log entry: we're standardizing on Vitest instead of Jest across the frontend
need an architecture decision record documenting the switch to a single MCP server, MADR format pls
we agreed to use Kafka for the ingestion pipeline in standup — write that up as an ADR with the alternatives we weighed
record why we chose to keep dist/ committed instead of building in CI, include consequences
draft a new adr in docs/adr/ for adopting feature flags via LaunchDarkly
just merged the auth rewrite — I want a proper record of the tradeoffs behind moving to OIDC, context and options and all
review ADR-0031 and tell me if it's ready to accept
audit the ADR corpus for dangling references and numbering holes
what architecture decisions are we missing docs for?
ratify ADR-0029 and flip it to accepted with today's date
where's the ADR that explains the branching model? point me to it
explain how the verify tool resolves gates from .marvin/config.json
queries.note: explain existing code → explain, not a decision record

queries.text: supersede ADR-0007 with the new working-directory decision and pair the links
regenerate the Architecture decisions digest in CLAUDE.md from the accepted records
plan the migration from the four-pack layout to the single plugin, with steps and rollback
queries.note: migration planning → migration-plan (the ADR records the choice; this plans the execution)

queries.text: summarize what architecture changed between v0.6.0 and v0.13.0 for the release notes
we decided on the new caching layer — write the decision record, don't just review an existing one
queries.note: shares ADR vocab with adr-review but the action is to DRAFT a new record

queries.text: capture the rationale for choosing Terraform over Pulumi as a new architecture decision record
queries.note: must beat docs-search — authoring a new ADR, not locating one

queries.text: do a readiness review of the ADR I just drafted before I accept it
queries.note: reviewing an already-drafted record is adr-review's job, not drafting

queries.text: lint the whole docs/adr/ folder and flag broken supersede pairs and placeholder residue
queries.note: corpus-wide lint is adr-audit, not authoring a single record
